title: salix × stottii
intro: Salix × stottii, also known as Stott's willow, is a hybrid species of willow tree, resulting from the crossing of Salix × sepulcralis and Salix × rubens. The bark and leaves of Stott's willow contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a potential source of medicinal benefits. The medicinal properties of Stott's willow have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including pain, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Stott's willow is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis, skin irritations, and wounds.
